questions.prompt: Which statement best reflects Oregon CCB licensing expectations across business entity types?
choices: Entity type changes setup steps, but covered compensated construction work still requires proper CCB licensing
Only corporations need a CCB license
Sole proprietors are exempt from licensing if they have written contracts
LLCs can delay licensing until after the first paid project
questions.explanation: CCB and ORS Chapter 701 requirements apply based on covered compensated construction activity, regardless of entity type.

questions.prompt: When choosing a business structure, which exam-relevant compliance check should be confirmed first?
choices: That the license is tied to the correct business record and kept active
That the company already has a branded website
That all invoices use a time-and-materials format
That every client prepays in full
questions.explanation: A valid active license mapped to the correct business identity is a core compliance control in Oregon contractor operations.

questions.prompt: Which source is most direct for Oregon contractor setup guidance by business type?
choices: Oregon CCB guide to becoming a licensed contractor
A private social media group for contractors
questions.explanation: CCB's licensing guide is the official, Oregon-specific source for setup workflows and baseline requirements.
